_comment: YOU PROBABLY DON'T NEED THIS FILE. A paired hub pulls your authored watches from the cloud on every device sync — describe one in the Hearth app and it starts running here within about a second; this file is then rewritten with the cloud's set as an offline cache (so a reboot with no internet keeps running them). Hand-write a watch here only to run the hub UNPAIRED, or to test without the app. `compiledSpec` is exactly what Qwen emits.
_inputs: Reference an input as `<nodeId>.<sensorKey>` (e.g. `node-a1b2.board.temp`) — the same id the cloud uses. A bare `board.temp` still resolves IF only one node reports it; with two such nodes the hub warns and reads no-data rather than firing on whichever reported last. Your node id prints as `id node-XXXX` on boot and in `curl http://localhost:8899/nodes`.
_calibrate: Run the node, note the idle board.temp, set `right` a few degrees above it, then cup the chip in your hand to cross it.
title: Warn me if the board gets hot
notify: The board just crossed the threshold ({detail}).
